name: FUEL
post_apply_message:
FUEL (2009, Codemasters, non-Steam cracked copy) — Apply auto-adds the Steam shortcut on GE-Proton 10.34. Stage GE first with 'gfm stage-runner'.

THE KEY BIT — THE RIGHT EXE: the shortcut points at SecuLauncher.exe, NOT FUEL.exe. Straight from the crack's own instructions (_HOW TO RUN GAME!!.txt by ChemicalFlood): "To Play The Game, right click and run SecuLauncher.exe as administrator — you cannot launch Fuel.exe as requires Windows Live Launcher." FUEL.exe is still gated behind Games for Windows Live (dead since 2014); SecuLauncher.exe is the crack's way in. If you ever re-add this game by hand, do NOT point it at FUEL.exe — it will not start.

GFWL — STILL UNSOLVED, AND HERE'S WHAT'S BEEN RULED OUT. FUEL is a Games for Windows Live game. Your copy already ships the GFWL stub (xlive.dll + xlive.ini in the game root) and the shortcut carries WINEDLLOVERRIDES="xlive=n,b" to make Wine load it. Both are correct. It still prompts for the GFWL launcher.

RULED OUT 2026-07-20: the stub is NOT the wrong build. Tony downloaded PCGW's 'Fuel GFWL Fix (XliveLess)' and it is BYTE-IDENTICAL to what the crack already ships — sha256 2239e7c6d22ace00ed44f48518cebcd31deac2c6e07629decf98a3899690ea25, 348160 bytes, dated 2014-05-18 (i.e. xliveless_20140518_v2), matching ini too. So there is nothing to replace and no file to stage.

ALSO RULED OUT: the launch option not sticking (verified written with Steam closed), and SecuROM (the symptom is a GFWL prompt, not a SecuROM error — the Razor SecuLauncher crack works).

ITERATION 4 — THE LIKELY CULPRIT: THE LAUNCHER PROCESS HOP (2026-07-20). Tony asked why we weren't using SecuLauncher — we are, and that question is what cracked it. SecuLauncher.exe is a LAUNCHER: it starts, then spawns FUEL.exe as a SEPARATE process. WINEDLLOVERRIDES is an environment variable, so Steam applies it to the process IT starts — SecuLauncher — which is the one process that doesn't need xlive at all. If SecuLauncher hands its child a rebuilt environment block (launchers routinely do), the override never reaches FUEL.exe, the process that actually loads xlive.dll. That fits every symptom: correct stub, correct option, verified written, still prompts.

So this recipe now sets the override IN THE PREFIX (wine_registry -> HKCU\Software\Wine\DllOverrides, xlive = native,builtin) instead of relying on the env var. A prefix override applies to EVERY process in the prefix regardless of who spawned it or what environment it was handed — which is exactly the gap. The launch option is left in place too; belt and braces, and it costs nothing.

The step is optional:true because it needs the prefix to exist — so run the game ONCE through Steam (let it fail at the GFWL prompt, that's fine, the prefix gets created), then re-apply this recipe, then launch again.

IF IT STILL PROMPTS, confirm with logging — set the launch option to:
    PROTON_LOG=1 WINEDLLOVERRIDES="xlive=n,b" %command%
launch, quit, then grep ~/steam-<appid>.log for xlive. If xlive.dll still never loads, it's a search-path problem (next: protontricks the stub INTO the prefix, which is what the Deck guide's commenters resorted to). If it DOES load and the game still prompts, the stub isn't satisfying this build and the remaining lead is the modified FUEL.exe those same comments mention.

FUEL is a genuinely nasty GFWL+SecuROM case — low payoff, so parking it is a perfectly reasonable call.

IF IT WON'T LAUNCH: install everything in the game's _Redist folder (4 programs incl. dotNetFx35 + DirectX) — the crack readme's fix for missing-DLL errors. Under Proton most is already provided by GE, so try without first. There's also a 3.7MB d3d9.dll wrapper in the folder (the *_d3d9.log files are its output); it's left to Proton's DXVK by default — only override d3d9=n,b if rendering is broken.

CONTROLLER + WIDESCREEN: this is a 2009 Codemasters racer — native widescreen and native Xbox 360 pad support, so Steam Input just works. Nothing to mod.

FOV: FUEL's field of view is on the tight side at 16:10; PCGW lists FOV as an area of interest for this game. Nothing bundled — say the word if it bothers you and we'll look.
notes:
Built 2026-07-13 straight off Tony's Windows install at F:\games\FUEL (585 files / 3.84GB), staged to NAS _games/FUEL — no SD scan needed.

LAUNCH TARGET IS THE WHOLE POINT: root exes are FUEL.exe (6.8MB), GameSetup.exe (0.5MB) and SecuLauncher.exe (0.1MB). The crack's readme is explicit that FUEL.exe requires the Windows Live launcher and CANNOT be used; SecuLauncher.exe is the entry point. Razor 1911 release (rzr-fuel.nfo: Codemasters, 2009-07-02, protection SecuROM); 1911.dll at the root is Razor's crack component. Do not 'tidy' the shortcut to FUEL.exe because it looks more canonical — it's the one that doesn't work.

_Redist holds 4 installers for missing-DLL errors per the readme. GFWL is dead (services off 2014) which is exactly why the FUEL.exe path is a dead end even on Windows.

GFWL FIX — ITERATION 2 (2026-07-19): the WINEDLLOVERRIDES="xlive=n,b" launch option was verified APPLIED (log: Steam closed, shortcut written with the option, restarted) but FUEL STILL hit the 'use the GFWL launcher' prompt. So the override is necessary but not sufficient — the crack's bundled xlive.dll (340KB) is the wrong build. Community consensus (PCGW 'Fuel GFWL Fix (XliveLess)' file 2392, FUEL Deck guide 3007886375, Lucis Caelum's save guide 268031297): use xliveless_20140518_v2 (~172KB) specifically. Good news from the symptom: 'GFWL' not 'SecuROM' means the Razor SecuLauncher crack IS working — only GFWL remains.

ITERATION 3 — 'WRONG XLIVELESS BUILD' THEORY DISPROVEN (2026-07-20). I had added an optional copy_files step to overlay PCGW's xliveless onto the SD copy, on the theory the crack shipped a wrong/older build (I read the PCGW page's '172.86 kB' as the DLL size — it's the ZIP's COMPRESSED size; the DLL inside is 348160 bytes). Tony downloaded it: sha256 2239e7c6d22ace00ed44f48518cebcd31deac2c6e07629decf98a3899690ea25 — BYTE-IDENTICAL to the crack's, ini identical too. The step was removed; there is nothing to stage and no payload to drop.

So all three easy explanations are dead: right stub present, override applied and verified written, SecuROM bypassed. What remains is whether Wine actually LOADS the game-folder stub — diagnose with PROTON_LOG=1 and grep the log for xlive (see post_apply_message). If it never loads, install the stub into the prefix via protontricks (what the Deck guide's commenters resorted to); if it loads and the game still prompts, the lead is the modified FUEL.exe they also mention. Low ROI — fine to park. Non-Steam -> computed appid (not in the gospel registry). Native widescreen + 360 pad (2009 Codemasters racer) so no controller/widescreen work needed. Copy speed note: 585 files/3.84GB staged at ~95 MB/s.
